MOTION ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH LOCAL RULE 2.7(B) (COURTESY COPIES); WITH COMPLIANCE, MOTION MAY BE RE-SET FOR HEARING. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF CHELSEA PACIFIC HOLDINGS, LP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHIFT ITS BURDEN AND SHOW THE ABSENCE OF TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT WITH COMPETENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. FOR EXAMPLE, FACTS NUMBER 3 AND 4 ARE SUPPORTED BY ONLY 2 SPECIFIC PAGES OF EX. A TO FAIDI DECLARATION. EVEN IF THE BURDEN WAS SHIFTED, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT, INCLUDING FACTS 3, 4 AND 6. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN ACCORDANCE WITH CCP 437C(G) AND WITH A STATEMENT RE: COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Tyler K. Marsten; Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP; 408-606-6300; appearing as counsel for Plaintiff Chealsea Pacific Holdings, LP.
Jessica Ponce Merino; Law Offices of Jessica Ponce; 213-263-2911; appearing as counsel for Defendant Clifford Fortuno.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CHELSEA PACIFIC HOLDINGS, LP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show the absence of triable issues of fact with competent admissible evidence. For example, facts number 3 and 4 are supported by only 2 specific pages of Ex. A to Faidi declaration. Even if the burden was shifted, there are triable issues of fact, including facts 3, 4 and 6.

Prevailing party to prepare order in accordance with CCP 437c(g) and with a statement re: compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
